text:
Market today: Tech is bouncing back after a rough chip selloff - headline 8 confirms the rebound. But the recovery is fragile; energy cost legislation for AI data centers and ongoing chip volatility are the real undercurrents for your names.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem - down nearly 13% and it's your largest position, so on margin that pain is amplified; watch that $336 stop hard. PLTR is also down over 10% with a SELL flag - no reason to hold a loser with that signal. CRWD is off 10%+ too but still on HOLD, so give it room to breathe toward that $590 stop. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record - one bad day could gap through your $150 stop; size it tiny on margin.

Watch: Whether today's chip rebound has legs - one more down day flips this into a real trend. Also track that AI energy cost bill; it directly hits your data center-heavy names.

Bottom line: Protect AVGO first - it's your largest position, it's losing, and margin turns a bad trade into a dangerous one.
